interpretation: ISCA1 encodes a mitochondrial protein involved in the assembly of iron-sulfur clusters, which are critical for the function of various enzymes and proteins within the mitochondria. While there is no direct evidence linking ISCA1 to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), its role in mitochondrial function suggests a potential indirect connection, as mitochondrial dysfunction is implicated in various bone diseases and may contribute to the pathogenesis of osteonecrosis. The retrieved evidence primarily relates to ISCA1's involvement in mitochondrial dysfunction syndromes, which could be relevant to the metabolic disturbances seen in SONFH. However, the lack of specific studies directly connecting ISCA1 to SONFH limits the strength of this association. Therefore, while ISCA1 may have biological relevance, its potential as a biomarker for early detection of SONFH is weak due to insufficient direct evidence.
relevance_summary: ISCA1 is involved in mitochondrial function, which may be indirectly relevant to the pathophysiology of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head.

result.title: ISCA1 (Q9BUE6)
result.text:
Protein: Iron-sulfur cluster assembly 1 homolog, mitochondrial
Function: Involved in the maturation of mitochondrial 4Fe-4S proteins functioning late in the iron-sulfur cluster assembly pathway. Probably involved in the binding of an intermediate of Fe/S cluster assembly

result.title: ISCA1 ↔ fatal multiple mitochondrial dysfunctions syndrome
result.text:
Disease: fatal multiple mitochondrial dysfunctions syndrome
Description: Multiple mitochondrial dysfunctions syndrome describes a group of rare inborn errors of energy metabolism due to defects in mitochondrial [4Fe-4S] protein assembly. Patients present with a neonatal/infancy onset of metabolic lactic acidosis (that may be associated with hyperglycinemia and other abnormal metabolic testing results), muscular hypotonia, absence of psychomotor development or developmental regression, as well as abnormal neuroimaging findings (including leukodystrophy, brain developmental defects, white matter abnormalities, cerebral atrophy), and other variable clinical features (e.g., optic atrophy, cardiomyopathy, pulmonary hypertension, seizures, and dysmorphic features). Early fatal outcome is usual.
Association score: 0.335

result.title: ISCA1
result.text:
Iron-sulfur cluster assembly 1 homolog, mitochondrial is an evolutionarily highly conserved protein for the biogenesis of iron-sulfur cluster across species. In humans it is encoded by the ISCA1 gene.

Nuclear prelamin A recognition factor like is a protein that in humans is encoded by the NARFL gene.

result.title: Chromosome 9
result.text:
Chromosome 9 is one of the 23 pairs of chromosomes in humans. Humans normally have two copies of this chromosome, as they normally do with all chromosomes. Chromosome 9 spans about 138 million base pairs of nucleic acids (the building blocks of DNA) and represents between 4.0 and 4.5% of the total DNA in cells.

Genes
Number of genes
These are some of the gene count estimates of human chromosome 9. Because researchers use different approaches to genome annotation, their predictions of the number of genes on each chromosome varies (for technical details, see gene prediction). Among various projects, the collaborative consensus coding sequence project (CCDS) takes an extremely conservative strategy. So CCDS's gene number prediction represents a lower bound on the total number of human protein-coding genes.

Gene list
The following is a partial list of genes on human chromosome 9. For a complete list, see the link in the infobox on the right.
